title: The Cryonic Woman

jokes.text: We come in peace. Tough luck!
jokes.punchline: Immediate hostile rejection
jokes.explanation: Clean subversion of sci-fi tropes with perfect economy. Two words destroy the expectation.

jokes.text: I left them onboard. I mean, what?!
jokes.punchline: Realizes mid-answer this reveals her incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Leela's self-awareness catching up to her words

jokes.text: Hey, Fry! I'm steering with my ass! The best thing I ever saw!
jokes.setup: Robot presumably using unconventional body part for ship control
jokes.punchline: Fry's enthusiastic approval of this ridiculous sight
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - only Bender would steer with his ass and only Fry would find it amazing

jokes.text: I should fire you three right now... but I'm just not that cold-hearted. You're all fired!
jokes.setup: Professor claims he's not cold-hearted enough to fire them
jokes.punchline: Immediately fires them anyway
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor contradiction - sets up mercy then delivers cruelty with perfect character consistency

jokes.text: Good riddance! Now Zoidberg is the popular one! Yes! Let's all talk to Zoidberg.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg celebrates being the popular one with others gone
jokes.punchline: His desperate plea reveals he's still not actually popular
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg moment - his delusion of popularity immediately undercut by his desperate need for attention

jokes.text: Oh, you know... What about money? All I've got is my first mustache... and a picture of my old girlfriend, Michelle... and that ski instructor who's just her friend.
jokes.punchline: The third item reveals his girlfriend was cheating
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure - builds from pathetic possessions to heartbreaking revelation with perfect Fry obliviousness

jokes.text: Care to donate to the anti-mugging-you fund?
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemism for extortion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that reframes mugging as charity work - highly quotable and original

jokes.text: A robbery of Fort Knox on elephant back? Dumbest thing I ever heard.
jokes.setup: Need money, someone suggests a plan
jokes.punchline: The plan is so ridiculous it's dismissed immediately
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual concept delivered matter-of-factly. The elephant detail makes it memorable

jokes.text: I tried to give you one and you ran away? It's how we met? Then what happened? Just give me your hand. Baby.
jokes.punchline: Fry's complete lack of memory about their origin story
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the pilot that highlights Fry's obliviousness about major life events

jokes.text: I even saved your poster of a chimp expressing your distaste for Mondays. He lives for the weekend.
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily profound interpretation of a simple poster
jokes.explanation: Funny parody of motivational poster culture and over-interpretation of simple imagery

jokes.text: Calcutta, we have a problem. Delivery boy? I must have mixed up the chips.
jokes.punchline: The 'problem' is just a minor administrative mix-up
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic reference for mundane workplace problem

jokes.text: They're okay. Then we'll redecorate your office.
jokes.punchline: Mild response triggers complete office redesign
jokes.explanation: Absurd overreaction to neutral response about Mondays shows bureaucratic extremism

jokes.text: I wanted to meet Shakespeare and I figured time was cyclical. Nope. Straight line.
jokes.setup: Person explains romantic reason for time travel
jokes.punchline: Fry's blunt crushing of their hopes with scientific fact
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery destroys romantic time travel fantasy with brutal efficiency

jokes.text: The probulator's done. That wasn't so bad. Oh, wait, it hasn't started yet. Okay, it's about to start.
jokes.punchline: Multiple false endings build dread
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of tension through false relief - familiar medical anxiety trope

jokes.text: Relax, chum. I'm not really a giant fly. I'm a horrible robot! Kill all humans!
jokes.setup: Bender tries to comfort someone scared of his disguise
jokes.punchline: His 'reassuring' truth is actually more terrifying
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender - tries to be helpful but makes things worse through his fundamental nature

jokes.text: They'll find a cure in the future. We have a cure now! Good. Then you won't mind if I use this.
jokes.setup: Fry suggests waiting for future medicine
jokes.punchline: When told cure exists now, he uses future device anyway
jokes.explanation: Fry's illogical preference for future tech over practical present solutions

jokes.text: Pizza delivery for... I.C. Wiener. Very funny. Now cough up the $12.95.
jokes.punchline: Delivery guy recognizes prank but still demands payment
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to pilot episode's inciting incident, though the prank name itself is juvenile

jokes.text: It's only been 23 minutes, you dumb cannoli! You got a problem, let's take it outside! Okay. I'll fight the box. I can take him.
jokes.punchline: Fry's willingness to physically fight a pizza box
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry logic - misses the obvious human opponent to fight the inanimate object

jokes.text: Oh, my God, it's Pauly Shore! All right! I loved you in Bio-Dome. You sure made trouble in that bubble.
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm for universally panned movie
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's terrible taste in entertainment matching his era

jokes.text: If it rhymes, I can cause trouble in it.
jokes.punchline: Shore's commitment to his rhyming troublemaker persona
jokes.explanation: Decent parody of Shore's comedy style, though relies on audience familiarity

jokes.text: While researching for the role, I ran computer simulations demonstrating... that the whole concept is fundamentally flawed. Be it expressed via dome, sphere, cube... or even the stately tetrahedron, buddy.
jokes.setup: Shore explaining his research for a dumb movie
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated analysis delivered in his distinctive style
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - giving Shore unexpectedly intelligent dialogue while maintaining his speech patterns

jokes.text: During Encino Man, my intellectual curiosity re: cryogenics was peaked. I resolved to freeze the weasel.
jokes.setup: Shore explaining his motivation for freezing himself
jokes.punchline: Academic language mixed with his signature slang
jokes.explanation: Continues the joke of Shore speaking intelligently while using his catchphrases

jokes.text: I was supposed to be unfrozen... for the 1000th anniversary screening of Jury Duty II. How come I'm not there? I woke you up early to hang out and do stuff.
jokes.punchline: Fry's casual disruption of millennium anniversary for friendship
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Shore's grand plans and Fry's simple desires

jokes.text: Fry? Is it really you? I don't know. Is it really you? What do you mean? Are you you, or not? Who wants to know?
jokes.punchline: Philosophical confusion derails romantic moment
jokes.explanation: Fry's inability to handle simple identity confirmation in emotional moment

jokes.text: His law school was so prestigious, the basketball coach... was Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
jokes.punchline: Supreme Court Justice as basketball coach
jokes.explanation: Absurd image of elderly Supreme Court Justice coaching basketball shows extreme prestige

jokes.text: I turned to the one thing able to lessen my pain: A carnival. But a corn dog bone got lodged in the control of the Spizzler... and I had to ride it for 8 hours.
jokes.setup: Turning to carnival for emotional relief
jokes.punchline: Trapped on ride for 8 hours due to food debris
jokes.explanation: Absurd carnival mishap with specific details makes it memorable and believable

jokes.text: I turned to the one thing able to lessen my pain: / A carnival.
jokes.setup: Michelle seeking comfort after marital problems
jokes.punchline: Carnival as therapy is unexpectedly specific and odd
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - expecting drugs/alcohol but getting carnival

jokes.text: Same reason they kept me out of school. No one cared I was gone? Not really. Except for one person.
jokes.setup: Fry learning nobody missed him when he disappeared
jokes.punchline: Exception raises hope before being left hanging
jokes.explanation: Sets up emotional beat about Fry's abandonment with cliffhanger structure

jokes.text: Now people like art that's tattooed on fat guys. I'm on loan from the Louvre.
jokes.punchline: Person IS the art on loan from famous museum
jokes.explanation: Brilliant concept of human as living artwork with institutional backing

jokes.text: Og, gorilla emperor of Earth!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to get their jobs back
jokes.punchline: Introduces himself as gorilla emperor
jokes.explanation: Ridiculous fake identity that fits Fry's poor planning and grandiose thinking

jokes.text: For one beautiful night, I knew what it was to be a grandmother. Subjugated, yet honored.
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific and contradictory grandmother experience
jokes.explanation: Absurd fantasy fulfillment with unexpectedly complex emotional description

jokes.text: For one beautiful night, I knew what it was to be a grandmother. / Subjugated, yet honored.
jokes.punchline: His oddly specific take on grandmother status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Professor line - bizarre, specific, and emotionally complex

jokes.text: With Fry fired, I can leave my jigsaw puzzles out. He won't eat the pieces.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining benefits of Fry being gone
jokes.punchline: Reveals Fry eats jigsaw puzzle pieces
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail that explains both Fry's stupidity and Professor's frustration

jokes.text: So, as Leela was saying, Fry can go shove a big, old-- Hey, Fry!
jokes.punchline: Fry appears at the worst possible moment
jokes.explanation: Classic awkward timing comedy with predictable but effective execution

jokes.text: Dating your ex, Fry? Have you lost all self-respect? All what?
jokes.punchline: Fry doesn't understand the concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - completely lacks self-awareness about lacking self-respect

jokes.text: It's different now. She was demanding and possessive. Now she wants me to do stuff, be with her.
jokes.setup: Fry claiming his ex has changed
jokes.punchline: His examples prove she hasn't changed at all
jokes.explanation: Fry's complete inability to recognize that the behavior he describes is exactly the same

jokes.text: Because he didn't in your time. True. But I used to fit in really well. Then good luck, sister.
jokes.setup: Michelle lamenting that Fry fits in but she doesn't
jokes.punchline: Amy's brutal assessment and dismissal
jokes.explanation: Amy's blunt honesty cuts through Michelle's self-pity with surgical precision

jokes.text: I can't find vanishing cream that won't make me really vanish.
jokes.punchline: Future cosmetics are too literal
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay about future technology being too advanced/literal

jokes.text: I thought you meant a motel where the bed is shaped like stuff.
jokes.setup: Michelle suggests romantic escape to future time
jokes.punchline: Fry interprets it as novelty motel
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Fry's lowbrow interpretation of romantic gestures

jokes.text: Why must you analyze everything with your relentless logic?
jokes.punchline: Accusing FRY of being too logical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant irony - Michelle accusing the least logical character of being too analytical

jokes.text: Michelle, I don't regret this. But I rue and lament it.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to express complex feelings about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Uses synonyms thinking they're different emotions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry vocabulary misunderstanding - thinks he's making subtle distinctions between synonyms

jokes.text: I miss 3000 when brains flew through space and people ate lasers.
jokes.punchline: Year 3000 had absurdly futuristic elements
jokes.explanation: Absurd but memorable imagery of an even more advanced future era

jokes.text: It'd be deeper, but I'm standing on a gopher.
jokes.setup: Michelle complaining about shallow hole for shelter
jokes.punchline: Fry's matter-of-fact explanation involves standing on wildlife
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd excuse delivered with Fry's characteristic matter-of-fact tone

jokes.text: I'm Butch, leader of this place! I took your hole. You can't do nothing about it!
jokes.punchline: He's a small child claiming ownership of a hole in the ground
jokes.explanation: Child gang leader trope with absurd stakes - fighting over a hole

jokes.text: I'm tired of the chief's girlfriend... lording it over me with her fancy coyote hide.
jokes.setup: Michelle expressing frustration with primitive society
jokes.punchline: Jealous of incredibly basic 'luxury' item
jokes.explanation: Michelle's materialistic nature persists even in post-apocalyptic setting

jokes.text: Death rolling. What? It's like skateboarding. Except half the time, someone dies. So it's safer than skateboarding.
jokes.punchline: Fry's statistical analysis makes it seem safer than normal activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expectations with perfect Fry logic that makes deadly activity sound reasonable

jokes.text: My mother always said you were a loser. Now go prove her wrong! Beth said that?
jokes.setup: Michelle trying to motivate Fry before dangerous challenge
jokes.punchline: Fry focuses on wrong part of the insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry reaction - gets distracted by personal slight instead of focusing on life-threatening situation

jokes.text: I won! I'm the new chief! I'm the tallest and I weigh the most! Hand it over, you giant idiot!
jokes.setup: Child celebrates victory and claims physical superiority
jokes.punchline: Calls much larger adult 'giant idiot' while claiming to be biggest
jokes.explanation: Basic irony of small child claiming physical dominance over adult

jokes.text: Butch, your mom's here! Come on, kids! You're late for Hebrew school! I don't wanna go. Every Monday and Wednesday's the same thing.
jokes.punchline: They're just kids being called home by their mothers for religious school
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of post-apocalyptic tropes - reveals 'wasteland warriors' are suburban kids

jokes.text: You were a loser in the year 2000, you're a loser in the year 4000. But in 3000, I had it all. Friends, a low-paying job, a bed in a robot's closet! I envied no man! But you wrecked everything!
jokes.setup: Michelle calling Fry a loser across time periods
jokes.punchline: Fry's definition of 'having it all' is pathetically modest
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Fry's character - his incredibly low standards for happiness and success

jokes.text: You were in Pauly Shore's tube. They delivered it to his movie screening. / They noticed you weren't him and threw you out.
jokes.setup: Explanation of how Fry ended up in wrong place
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal from Pauly Shore event
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that resolves the plot confusion

jokes.text: So these aren't the decaying ruins of New York in 4000? You wish. You're in Los Angeles!
jokes.setup: Fry hoping the wasteland is future New York
jokes.punchline: It's present-day Los Angeles
jokes.explanation: Classic LA-bashing joke - current LA is indistinguishable from post-apocalyptic wasteland

jokes.text: The people are phony. No one reads. Everything has cilantro on it--
jokes.punchline: Escalates from serious social criticism to petty food complaint
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from legitimate criticisms to trivial personal preferences

jokes.text: Tis better to have loved and lost, n'est-ce pas?
jokes.punchline: Mixes English poetry with French phrase incorrectly
jokes.explanation: Zoidberg's pretentious attempt at wisdom with mismatched languages

jokes.text: I've forgotten why I even fired you. He destroyed your business and all your possessions. Oh, that's right. Get lost!
jokes.setup: Professor genuinely can't remember why he fired them
jokes.punchline: Reminded of massive destruction, immediately rejects them again
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Professor's forgetfulness and quick mood changes when reminded

tmdb_overview: Fry is reunited with his old girlfriend from the 20th Century, but she doesn't adapt to the 31st century as well as he has. Convincing Fry to return to the cryogenic chamber, they awaken in a barren wasteland and struggle to make a place for themselves.
